name: Easter Mints Kids Can Make
recipeInstructions:
1. This is a no-cook recipe the children can mix with their hands.
2. Flavor it with any of the liquid flavorings in the supermarket, such as strawberry and lemon. Alternatively, divide the mixture into three portions and add a few drops of food coloring to tint them yellow, red, and green.
3. Help the children measure all the ingredients into a large bowl.
4. They can take turns stirring it with a wooden spoon until it becomes too stiff.
5. Then they can knead the dough with their hands until it is smooth.
6. Give each child a paper plate and a pencil. Instruct them to turn their plates over and write their names on the bottom to prevent pencil lead from getting on their mints.
7. Make sure they use upper and lower case letters when writing their names.
8. Give each child a portion of dough on his or her plate.
9. Children can pinch off pieces, roll them into balls, and press them lightly with a fork to make fancy butter mints. Those who cannot roll the candy into balls can make snakes, cut the snakes into pieces, and press the pieces with a fork.
10. Leave the mints on the plates and refrigerate them for 30 minutes until they become firm.
11. Easter Mints taste even better the second day if everyone can resist eating them all on the first day.
12. Cover with plastic wrap and keep in the refrigerator.
